mission.title: vllm: GGUF support
mission.description: Enable GGUF model format support in vLLM for flexible quantization control. GGUF allows fine-grained control over bit depth and VRAM usage compared to other quantization formats like AWQ. Requested by 56+ users.

steps.title: Check current vLLM setup
steps.description:
Verify your vLLM version and configuration:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
# or
python -c "import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)"
```
GGUF support requires vLLM v0.3.0 or later.

steps.title: Install or upgrade vLLM with GGUF support
steps.description:
Ensure you have a version of vLLM that includes GGUF support:
```bash
pip install vllm --upgrade
```
vLLM's GGUF support loads quantized weights and dequantizes them to fp16 for matrix multiplication. This gives you control over VRAM usage by choosing different quantization bit depths (e.g., Q2_K, Q4_K, Q5_K).

steps.title: Obtain a GGUF model
steps.description:
Download a pre-quantized GGUF model from HuggingFace or convert one yourself:
```bash
# Example: convert a GGUF file to a torch-compatible format
PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION=python python convert_gguf_to_torch.py \
  --input mixtral-8x7b-instruct-v0.1.Q2_K.gguf \
  --output mixtral-q2k
```
Many popular models are available in GGUF format on HuggingFace (e.g., TheBloke's quantized models).

steps.title: Serve the GGUF model with vLLM
steps.description:
Start the vLLM OpenAI-compatible API server with a GGUF model:
```bash
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model <path-to-gguf-model> \
  --tokenizer <original-model-name>
```
The GGUF weights are dequantized to fp16 at load time, then standard vLLM inference runs on the dequantized weights.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that GGUF model serving is working:
```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "<model-name>", "prompt": "Hello, world!", "max_tokens": 50}'
```
You should receive a valid completion response from the GGUF-quantized model.

resolution.summary: vLLM added GGUF format support by implementing weight loading that dequantizes GGUF quantized weights to fp16 for matrix multiplication. This enables users to run GGUF-quantized models with vLLM's high-throughput serving engine while maintaining control over VRAM usage via quantization bit depth selection.
codeSnippets:
# Serve a GGUF model with vLLM
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model <path-to-gguf-model>

# Convert GGUF to torch format
PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION=python python convert_gguf_to_torch.py --input model.Q2_K.gguf --output model-q2k

prerequisites.description: Python 3.8+, pip, a CUDA-capable GPU, and a working vLLM installation (pip install vllm).
